auth_module_map.role: Defines AbstractBaseUser (lines 43-164) with core auth fields: username, password, last_login
Defines AbstractUser (lines 446-514) extending AbstractBaseUser + PermissionsMixin with personal/admin fields; UserManager (line 140) handles user creation
Initial migration schema defining User model structure and field constraints
User creation/change forms (lines 213-302) validate and handle user data; BaseUserCreationForm at line 213
ModelBackend (line 54) authenticates users; configure_user method at line 327 allows backend-specific user setup
check_user_model function (line 27) validates custom user models against auth requirements

migration_trace: 1. Define custom User model extending AbstractBaseUser (not AbstractUser) at app/models.py
2. Add custom fields: profile_tier, external_sso_id, last_sync_at to custom User model
3. Create UserManager subclass (models.py:140-239) to handle user creation with extra_fields for custom fields
4. Register custom User model in AUTH_USER_MODEL setting (Django checks.py:27 validates this)
5. Create initial migration in app/migrations/ with CreateModel for User + 3 custom fields (see migrations/0001_initial.py:13-204 for pattern)
6. Extend UserCreationForm (forms.py:213-251 BaseUserCreationForm pattern) to include custom fields in admin
7. Extend UserChangeForm (forms.py:275-302 UserChangeForm pattern) to display custom fields
8. Update backend.configure_user (backends.py:327) if custom fields need backend-specific initialization
9. Add custom validation in User.clean() (models.py:497-499 pattern) for profile_tier choices
10. Run makemigrations and migrate to deploy custom User model alongside auth framework

unmappable_fields.reason: CharField with choices=['free','pro','enterprise'] has no equivalent in AbstractBaseUser (line 43-164: only has username, password, last_login) or AbstractUser (line 446-514: adds first_name, last_name, email, is_staff, is_active, date_joined, groups, user_permissions but no tier/subscription field)
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add as custom CharField(max_length=20, choices=TIER_CHOICES) directly to custom User model; extend UserCreationForm/UserChangeForm (forms.py:213-302) to validate and render profile_tier field; add validation logic in User.clean() method (models.py:497 pattern)
unmappable_fields.evidence: models.py:446-514 (AbstractUser fields), models.py:468-484 (all built-in User fields), forms.py:213-251 (form extension pattern)

unmappable_fields.reason: CharField with unique=True, nullable has no equivalent in AbstractBaseUser or AbstractUser. Django auth manages authentication via username+password or backend-specific methods (backends.py:54-242 ModelBackend), not external SSO identifiers. EmailField (models.py:470) is the closest unique identifier but serves a different purpose.
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add custom CharField(max_length=255, unique=True, null=True, blank=True) to custom User model; handle SSO mapping in custom authentication backend by extending ModelBackend.authenticate() (backends.py:59-72) to check external_sso_id; implement custom RemoteUserBackend.configure_user() (backends.py:327-333) to populate external_sso_id on SSO user creation
unmappable_fields.evidence: backends.py:54-89 (ModelBackend.authenticate pattern), backends.py:327-333 (configure_user hook for SSO integration), models.py:470 (unique email field precedent)

unmappable_fields.reason: DateTimeField nullable has no equivalent in AbstractBaseUser (only has last_login at line 45) or AbstractUser. Django auth tracks last_login automatically (signals.py, models.py:18-24 update_last_login), but has no 'sync' timestamp. Custom sync tracking is application-specific, not framework-provided.
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add custom DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True) to custom User model; create signal handler connected to custom sync event (not Django's user_logged_in signal at models.py:5) to auto-update last_sync_at on external data sync operations; optional: add sync_now() method to User model to update timestamp on-demand
unmappable_fields.evidence: models.py:18-24 (update_last_login signal handler pattern using signals.py:5), base_user.py:45 (last_login DateTimeField as nearest precedent), models.py:484 (date_joined DateTimeField pattern)
